feed.title: Combined summary - [BIP Proposal] Informational BIP on Low-R Signature Grinding

entry.title: Combined summary - [BIP Proposal] Informational BIP on Low-R Signature Grinding
entry.summary: The proposed informational Bitcoin Improvement Proposal (BIP) aims to formally document the Low-R signature algorithm, which has been implemented by Bitcoin Core since 2018 through PR 1366 and is utilized by numerous wallets. This initiative is driven by the need for consistent implementation across all wallets to maintain privacy. The documentation would benefit from a concise structure that includes a collection of test vectors to guide developers. Sean Gilligan, who has been working on the secp256k1-jdk, a new Java wrapper for the secp256k1 library, highlighted the utility of referencing existing implementations such as the C++ version used in Bitcoin Core and rust-secp256k1 during development. The idea for the BIP documentation has garnered support from several developers and was initially suggested during discussions on PR 13666. In response to Sean's proposal, another developer, Murch, suggested not to overly focus on test vectors at the initial stages of drafting the BIP. According to Murch, these vectors are crucial but not necessary until the BIP progresses towards 'Complete' status. They can be added later either during the draft review process or after publishing. Following this advice, Sean confirmed plans to begin drafting the BIP, adhering to guidelines set out in BIP 3, including creating a PR against their fork of the BIPs repository. The documentation process for this BIP appears to be collaborative, with contributions expected from multiple developers. This approach not only enriches the content but also ensures comprehensive coverage and accuracy in the final document. The inclusion of test vectors will likely be a significant aspect of the BIP, emphasizing the practical application of the Low-R signature algorithm in various scenarios. For more detailed information on the Low-r grinding technique and its various implementations, you can refer to the Bitcoin Optech page on [Low-R Signature Grinding](https://bitcoinops.org/en/topics/low-r-signatures). For those interested in the development updates of the secp256k1-jdk project, further details can be found on the project repository [secp256k1-jdk](https://github.com/bitcoin-core/secp256k1).
